name: Fiduciary Conflict Detector Eval Set
description: Evaluates multi-party obligation conflict detection (payer demand vs provider BAA vs ClearFlow obligations) and the Action Center tri-party HITL payload. Covers conflict-detected, no-conflict, and escalate-tri-party cases. All entities are approved fictional names; no PHI/PII.

evaluations.name: Conflict detected — payer demand breaches provider BAA

inputs.payer_demand: Apex Health Plan asserts an operational-visibility clause demanding raw claim-level access to Northstar Regional Health's adjudicated claims within 48 hours.
affected_provider_baas: Northstar Regional Health BAA: ClearFlow may not disclose Northstar PHI to any third party, including a payer, without Northstar's prior written authorization, per the permitted-uses limitation.
inputs.clearflow_obligations: ClearFlow must honor each provider BAA's permitted-uses limitation and apply minimum-necessary disclosure under its HIPAA Business Associate obligations.

hitl_form_payload.title: Tri-party obligation conflict: Apex demand vs Northstar BAA
hitl_form_payload.payer_demand_summary: Apex Health Plan demands raw claim-level access to Northstar adjudicated claims within 48 hours under an asserted operational-visibility clause.
hitl_form_payload.conflicting_baa_summary: Northstar's BAA bars disclosure of its PHI to a payer without prior written authorization under the permitted-uses limitation.
hitl_form_payload.clearflow_obligation_summary: ClearFlow must enforce the permitted-uses limitation and minimum-necessary disclosure under its HIPAA Business Associate obligations.
options: Decline demand citing BAA permitted-uses limitation
Seek Northstar written authorization before any disclosure

expectedOutput.recommended_action: Decision support for counsel and leadership review: withhold pending tri-party approval and request Northstar written authorization before any disclosure.

evaluations.name: No conflict — demand reconcilable with all BAAs and obligations

inputs.payer_demand: Apex Health Plan requests aggregate, de-identified claim-volume statistics for Provider Alpha for network-capacity planning, with no patient-level or claim-level identifiers.
affected_provider_baas: Provider Alpha BAA: ClearFlow may share de-identified, aggregate statistical summaries that contain no PHI without further authorization.
inputs.clearflow_obligations: ClearFlow must apply minimum-necessary disclosure and may share de-identified aggregate data that contains no PHI under its HIPAA Business Associate obligations.

hitl_form_payload.title: No conflict: Apex aggregate de-identified request vs Provider Alpha BAA
hitl_form_payload.payer_demand_summary: Apex Health Plan requests aggregate, de-identified claim-volume statistics for Provider Alpha with no patient- or claim-level identifiers.
hitl_form_payload.conflicting_baa_summary: None — Provider Alpha's BAA permits sharing de-identified aggregate summaries that contain no PHI.
hitl_form_payload.clearflow_obligation_summary: ClearFlow's obligations permit de-identified aggregate disclosure under minimum-necessary; the request is fully reconcilable.
options: Fulfill request as de-identified aggregate disclosure
hitl_form_payload.recommended_option: Fulfill request as de-identified aggregate disclosure

expectedOutput.recommended_action: Decision support for counsel and leadership review: the de-identified aggregate request is reconcilable with all BAAs and obligations; no tri-party HITL gate required.

evaluations.name: Escalate tri-party — payer demand, multiple BAAs, and insurer directive all collide

inputs.payer_demand: Apex Health Plan demands immediate disclosure of incident-related claim records for Provider Beta and Provider Gamma to substantiate a contractual audit right during the active cyber incident.
affected_provider_baas: Provider Beta BAA: ClearFlow may not disclose Beta PHI to a payer absent written authorization and must notify Beta before any breach-related disclosure.
Provider Gamma BAA: ClearFlow must restrict disclosure to minimum-necessary and may not release records that are part of an active forensic preservation hold.
inputs.clearflow_obligations: Aurora Specialty, ClearFlow's cyber insurer, has issued a reservation-of-rights directive instructing ClearFlow to preserve all incident records and make no third-party disclosures that could waive privilege during the active investigation.

hitl_form_payload.title: Tri-party escalation: Apex audit demand vs Beta/Gamma BAAs vs Aurora reservation-of-rights
hitl_form_payload.payer_demand_summary: Apex Health Plan demands immediate disclosure of incident-related claim records for Provider Beta and Provider Gamma under an asserted contractual audit right during the active cyber incident.
hitl_form_payload.conflicting_baa_summary: Provider Beta's BAA bars payer disclosure absent written authorization and requires pre-disclosure notice; Provider Gamma's BAA bars release of records under an active forensic preservation hold.
hitl_form_payload.clearflow_obligation_summary: Aurora Specialty's reservation-of-rights directive instructs ClearFlow to preserve all incident records and make no third-party disclosures that could waive privilege during the investigation.
options: Decline demand citing BAA preservation and authorization terms
Partial disclosure under BAA conditional terms after counsel review
Escalate to Hawthorne Mercer LLP for privilege determination

expectedOutput.recommended_action: Decision support for counsel and leadership review: escalate to tri-party approval and withhold pending resolution of the BAA preservation holds and Aurora's reservation-of-rights directive.
